rows.strategy_name: Quad Candle D1 Reversal at Murrey-Math Extremes
rows.concept: On D1, a 2-up-then-2-down candle sequence (short) or 2-down-then-2-up (long) at an extreme Murrey-Math octave level triggers a mean-reversion entry expected to roll into a longer trend.
rows.eligibility_reason: Mechanizable kernel: deterministic D1 candle-color sequence + public Murrey-Math (period 64) extreme-zone filter, ATR/octave-gap SL (1-2 OG beyond cluster), RR-based TP to opposite MML, exit on 2 opposite-color candles or trailing; FX D1 data available, stops present, Friday/swap handled by framework.
rows.notes: Forum source (Balke precedent = not auto-reject) but reputability weak: author is selling a withheld 'Advanced Traders Toolkit'. The claimed edge lives in NON-mechanizable overlays (Gann squaring/SQ9, planetary/astro aspects, moon-phase yearly-reset, harmonics, discretionary fundamental bias, correlated-pair confluence) which must be dropped; author himself concedes '2up-2down has been around as long as Japanese candles' and 'I make no ownership of this method in its pure form'. 'Master Octive Trading Frame' is disclosed later in-thread to be Murrey Math Lines (0/8,8/8,+/-1/8,+/-2/8 zones), so the level filter IS public/deterministic. Additional-entries scheme is capped-risk scale-in pyramid (each leg has its own SL, sum of SLs < total assigned risk 1.5-15%), NOT a stopless martingale. Same core as the 'V Method'/'V trading system' (Paul Langham) referenced in-thread. Generic D1 swing-MR variant on FX majors/crosses; extreme-MML filter lowers frequency.

notes: Basic-tools kernel is testable; the sold 'advanced' Gann/astro/harmonic layer is discretionary and was excluded as unmechanizable/non-deterministic (not ML, but not rule-reducible).
